working: Email opens stay well above the 25-35% health benchmark: the three July 2 sends opened at 47.9% (Alumni), 49.6% (Staff-Client, 8,894 sends), and the account-wide average is 52.0% across 100 campaigns.
Best traffic drivers remain Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (+66.5% lift, 5/19) and Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (+65.2% lift, 6/3) -- both mid-month Tue/Wed sends.
SEO is accelerating: GSC clicks jumped 3 -> 20 and impressions 1,573 -> 3,173 vs the last run, with impression-weighted avg position improving from 31.6 to 17.6 over the 30-day window.
Referring Partners eNews - June 2026 has the best per-open engagement of the new sends: 4.17% click-to-open vs 1.4-1.5% for the Alumni and Staff-Client lists.
not_working: UTM leakage is total: all 10 tracked sends show email_sessions_3d = 0 while direct_sessions_3d runs 21-53, and Email is credited just 1 of 1,959 sessions (0.1%) in 90 days -- GA4 is mis-crediting every newsletter click to Direct.
All three July 2 sends show -25.3% lift, though the 3-day post-send window landed on the July 4 holiday (site totals of 8, 9, 14 sessions Jul 3-5), so the negative reading is confounded.
Click rates are weak across the board: 0.68% (Alumni July) and 0.73% (Staff-Client June) vs the ~2%+ benchmark; only Alumni eNews - May 2026 cleared it at 1.79%.
Referring Partners open rate (25.1%) is roughly half the other lists -- that list is stale or under-segmented.
Organic Social is still just 51 sessions (2.6% of mix), and window total sessions slipped 2,021 -> 1,959 vs the July 1 run.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email to all newsletter links -- with 0 email-attributed sessions across 10 sends, this is the single highest-value fix and would finally make lift measurable.
Schedule sends mid-month on Tue/Wed (the +66.5% and +65.2% lifts came 5/19 and 6/3) and avoid holiday-adjacent dates like the July 2 batch.
Repromote the Staff-Client May themes and reuse the Alumni May 2026 content style (1.79% click, 3.77% CTOR) with fewer, bolder CTAs to pull click rates toward 2%.
Clean or re-permission the Referring Partners list to close the 25% vs 48-50% open gap.
Keep publishing SEO pages while momentum holds -- impressions doubled and avg position improved 14 spots in 30 days; converting position ~17 rankings to page 1 is the nearest-term click win.
summary: 1,959 sessions in the 90-day window with Direct leading at 49.5%; email opens are strong (48-50%) but zero UTM attribution keeps Email at 0.1% of the mix, while GSC clicks jumped 3 to 20 with avg position improving to 17.6.
